ops.body:
# SnapshotWatcher: watch the parent directory, not the file

Consumers publish snapshots with an atomic rename-into-place, which replaces the inode — and a file-level (vnode) watch silently dies with the old inode. The watch is therefore placed on the PARENT directory, which survives the swap. Reloads are debounced (~200 ms default) to collapse the event burst a single write emits. Decoding tries ISO8601 WITH fractional seconds first, then plain ISO8601 (ISO8601Parser is @unchecked Sendable — sound because it is only used from the serial decode path; the formatters are reference types).

Every snapshot-file read/decode outcome is a typed SnapshotState case — `loaded` / `missing` / `malformed` / `versionSkew` — a bad file never crashes and never silently yields stale data. (`SnapshotWatcherError.cannotOpenDirectory` is separate: start() throws it when the parent directory cannot be opened for monitoring.) `SnapshotDecodeError` retains a String(describing:) of the underlying error — not the Error value itself — because a bare `any Error` is not Sendable. start() delivers the current state once immediately.

**Lifetime invariant:** dropping a started watcher cancels its dispatch source and closes the O_EVTONLY directory file descriptor — deinit is load-bearing cleanup, so the consumer must hold a strong reference for the intended life of the watch (the test suite pins this).

# ReloadCoalescer: the WidgetKit refresh budget

Widgets that reload on every state change exhaust the WidgetKit refresh budget. record(trigger:) coalesces fire-throughs to at most one per interval (default 5 min), LEADING edge: the first request in a window fires immediately, later ones drop. Deliberately does NOT import WidgetKit — the consumer supplies the closure calling WidgetCenter.shared.reloadTimelines; the `now` seam makes the window testable.
